description: Weather-safety infographic on a dark background showing a photographic night sky with multiple bright lightning bolts. At top-left in white text: 'Lightning is hotter than the surface of the Sun and can reach temperatures around.' On the right is the large white figure '50,000°F.' On the lower-left is an illustration of a thermometer with a red rising column over a cross-section of the layered orange and yellow interior of the Sun, with a small lightning-bolt icon at the bottom. A small orange caption at bottom-right reads 'When Thunder Roars Go Indoors' with 'weather.gov/lightning' beneath it.
notable_text: Lightning is hotter than the surface of the Sun and can reach temperatures around 50,000°F; When Thunder Roars Go Indoors; weather.gov/lightning
